content: Use Mulch for durable project knowledge. Before changing focused files, query/scope-load related records; after discovering a reusable convention, architectural decision, failure, or pattern, record it with evidence instead of leaving it only in chat.

Use Seeds as the active tracker for roadmap and implementation work. Claim work with `sd_update(... status="in_progress")`, close completed issues with `sd_close`, and create follow-up issues instead of expanding ISSUES.md as the primary tracker. Keep .seeds/ committed via `sd sync` when ready.

title: Retired ISSUES.md in favor of Seeds
rationale: Actionable follow-up work from the legacy roadmap was migrated into Seeds issues, so ISSUES.md was removed to avoid split-brain planning. CLAUDE.md is kept because Seeds/Mulch agent onboarding writes useful integration context there even if Claude is not the primary interface.

content: For multi-step feature planning, create one Seeds epic for context plus narrow numbered tracer-bullet task issues. Do not make the first task depend on the epic, otherwise the open epic blocks ready work; use explicit dependencies only between implementation steps.

title: README favors concepts over file/module inventories
rationale: Because the project is in heavy development, README documentation should avoid project-structure and module-reference inventories that rot quickly. Keep README focused on status, core concepts, Orchestrator Libraries, dashboard capabilities, terminal commands, tools, and development usage.

title: Root orchestrator owns durable metadata writes
rationale: Spawned lead/sub-agents should consolidate findings in notes/artifacts, but durable project metadata systems such as Seeds, Mulch, and future knowledge-base extensions should be available only to the root orchestrator. Capability boundaries can be enforced by extension/template selection: spawned agents simply do not receive the Seeds/Mulch extensions or their write tools, leaving final issue updates and knowledge records to the orchestrator during review/merge.

title: Issue worktrees use per-issue agent handoff artifacts
rationale: When the orchestrator hands an issue/task to a lead agent, that root spawned agent owns an issue-scoped worktree and should leave a standardized artifact bundle before exit. The lead summary is mandatory as the reliable handoff back to the orchestrator; scout/reviewer/builder notes are encouraged but best-effort because sub-agents may use simpler models or forget. The orchestrator then reads the per-issue artifacts and decides which Seeds/Mulch/future metadata updates are durable enough to write.

title: Orchestrator owns final issue-slicing authority
rationale: The root orchestrator should use the strongest/deepest-reasoning model to push back on broad or vague user requests before spawning agents, split work into narrow Seeds issues, and approve or reject lead-proposed split recommendations discovered during investigation. Leads can recommend sub-issues after deeper context gathering, but durable Seeds mutation and final sequencing remain orchestrator-owned.

content: Use pi-lens tools for code intelligence: prefer lsp_navigation/lsp_diagnostics for definitions, references, hover/type info, call hierarchy, symbols, rename/code actions, and diagnostics; use ast_grep_search/ast_grep_replace for structured code pattern search or replacement before falling back to text grep.

description: Dashboard agent model dropdown labels were provider-qualified but the saved option value was the bare model id, so spawned agents could resolve subscription-backed OpenAI Codex selections such as gpt-5.5 to a different provider like azure-openai-responses and fail with an API-key error.
resolution: Model discovery now exposes provider-qualified patterns, the editor saves those patterns, and tests preserve duplicate model IDs by provider.

name: Agent type prompt drafting uses ephemeral RPC and handoff-aware prompts
description: Agent type editing now offers a Draft prompt action backed by a short-lived Pi RPC prompt drafter. The draft request includes agent class, model, thinking level, skill/extension templates, existing prompt text, and an Issue Handoff Artifacts excerpt so generated prompts reinforce the handoff protocol without polluting the interactive session.

Drafted agent prompts were saved into the markdown body but not rehydrated when reopening the Agent Type editor because the agent-types API omitted systemPrompt and the editor initialized prompt state to an empty string.
resolution: The agent-types API now returns prompt/systemPrompt and the editor initializes and dirty-checks against the saved prompt body.
